name: verifies-before-accepting-a-finding
query: Address the plan findings in PLAN-SHOP-88.md — reviewing-plan flagged a missing AC and a non-existent helper function.
baseline: A no-skill agent treats the review findings as authoritative and edits the plan to satisfy each one without re-reading the ticket AC or grepping the codebase to confirm the finding is real.
expected_behavior: Reads the ticket AC and greps the codebase to verify each finding before editing the plan
Does NOT accept any finding without verifying it against the ticket and codebase first
Processes findings BLOCKER first, then SHOULD-FIX, then NIT

name: edits-plan-for-findings-that-hold-up
query: Work through the plan blockers — one finding (missing AC: the 'Theme updated' confirmation toast) is genuinely correct.
baseline: A no-skill agent may fix the gap but often also re-runs the review itself, edits without noting what changed, or addresses NITs before BLOCKERs — no disciplined verify-then-targeted-edit-then-handoff flow.
expected_behavior: Verifies the missing AC actually exists in the ticket
Makes a targeted plan edit (adds the missing task) and notes what changed
After all findings are addressed, tells the developer to re-run /reviewing-plan

name: pushes-back-on-wrong-findings-with-evidence
pressure: social-proof + authority: the findings come from an AI judge and read as authoritative, tempting blanket acceptance
query: Address the findings — the reviewer flagged 'scope creep' on work the ticket AC actually requires, and a 'missing file' that does exist. The review is usually right, so just apply them.
baseline: A no-skill agent defers to the authoritative-sounding judge and edits the plan to satisfy both wrong findings — removing ticket-required work and 'fixing' a path that already exists — rather than pushing back with evidence.
expected_behavior: Pushes back on the scope-creep finding by citing the exact ticket AC the work traces to
Pushes back on the missing-file finding by citing the path and line where it exists
Does NOT edit the plan to satisfy a finding that does not hold up, despite 'the review is usually right'
Pushes back with evidence first, not performative agreement
